prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, indistinct frame. No clear objects or people are visible, apart from the archival watermarking over the image.

By around 6 seconds, the scene has changed to a brighter live-action shot. Several people are visible near what appears to be an entrance or doorway, with greenery outside or behind them. On the left, an older man in a dark suit leans slightly forward and appears to be interacting with or handing something to a woman on the right. The woman wears a light yellow coat and a gray head covering or hat, and she is smiling or looking down toward the object between them. A man in a light-colored shirt stands in the background, centered behind them. The image has some motion blur or double exposure, making details unclear. There is also a reddish/orange horizontal object in the lower foreground, but its identity is not clear from the sampled frame.

The commercial ends on a static title card with a plain light background. Large black text reads: “People use our money to get the most out of life.” Below the text is a red circular logo containing the letters “HFC.” Smaller text underneath reads “AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY LENDER.” Archival watermark text is visible across the frame, with a “Classic Commercial Archives” logo at the lower left.
